spec.name: Foundation-Lite
spec.description: Foundation-Lite Centos 7 Blueprint that assumes DHCP configuration, ejects CDROM, runs yum update, disables SELinux and initializes data disk. Sends out an email notification on successful creation of VM.

action_list.name: action_create
action_list.description: System action for creating an application

attrs.script:
#!/bin/bash

# region headers
# * author:     nutanix
# * version:    v1.0/20210504 -  version
# task_name:    EjectCdrom
# description:  ejects all cdrom devices (/dev/sr*).               
# output vars:  none
# dependencies: none
# endregion

for device in /dev/sr*
do 
    sudo eject $device
    echo "Ejected ${device}"
done

# region headers
# * author:     nutanix
# * version:    v1.0/20210504 -  version
# task_name:    UpdateRoot
# description:  updates root password with configured Calm credential secret.               
# output vars:  none
# dependencies: requires the root credential to be present in the blueprint.
# endregion
echo 'root:@@{root.secret}@@' | sudo chpasswd

#!/bin/bash # region headers # * author: nutanix # * version: v1.0/20210504 - version # task_name: SendMail # description: sends an email notification to the Calm user including vm details. # output vars: none # dependencies: none # endregion sudo sed -i 's/MaxSessions 1/MaxSessions 10/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config sudo sed -i 's/#Subsystem/Subsystem/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config sudo /bin/systemctl restart sshd.service From=@@{email_sender}@@ To=@@{requester_email}@@ Subject="Your VM @@{vm_name}@@ is ready!" Body="

Hello @@{vm_requester_name}@@,

You had requested a Linux VM, we have successfully deployed your instance and you can start using it using the following informations:
Service type: Linux instance
Machine ip address: @@{address}@@
Instance name: @@{vm_name}@@
Login: @@{linux.username}@@
Password: none: using your private ssh key
For security reasons, please do not share your password or key with anyone.

Regards,

The Cloud Team

" echo " import email.message import smtplib server = smtplib.SMTP("\"@@{smtp_server}@@\"", 25) msg = email.message.Message() msg['Subject'] = "\"${Subject}\"" msg['From'] = "\"${From}\"" msg['To'] = "\"${To}\"" html_content="\"${Body}\"" msg.add_header('Content-Type', 'text/html') msg.set_payload(html_content) server.sendmail(msg['From'], [msg['To']], msg.as_string())" | tee ~/send_notification echo "Sending user notification" python ~/send_notification rm ~/send_notification

action_list.name: action_start
action_list.description: System action for starting an application

action_list.name: action_stop
action_list.description: System action for stopping an application

action_list.name: action_delete
action_list.description: System action for deleting an application. Deletes created VMs as well

action_list.name: action_restart
action_list.description: System action for restarting an application

attrs.script:
# region headers
# * author:     nutanix
# * version:    v1.0/20210504 -  version
# task_name:    Restart
# description:  schedules a reboot 1 minute from now.               
# output vars:  none
# dependencies: none
# endregion
sudo shutdown --reboot 1

action_list.name: action_soft_delete
action_list.description: System action for deleting an application. Does not delete created VMs

cloud_init.user_data:
#cloud-config
disable_root: False

hostname: @@{vm_name}@@
fqdn: @@{vm_name}@@.@@{domain}@@

users:
  - default
  - name: @@{linux.username}@@
    shell: /bin/bash
    homedir: /home/@@{linux.username}@@
    ssh-authorized-keys:
      - @@{public_key}@@
    sudo: ['ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL']
ssh_pwauth: False
package_upgrade: true

attrs.script:
#!/bin/bash

# region headers
# * author:     nutanix
# * version:    v1.0/20210504 -  version
# task_name:    DisableSelinux
# description:  disables enforcement of selinux policy.               
# output vars:  none
# dependencies: none
# endregion

sudo hostnamectl set-hostname --static @@{vm_name}@@
echo "@@{address}@@  @@{vm_name}@@.@@{domain}@@ @@{vm_name}@@" | sudo tee -a /etc/hosts

#sudo /bin/systemctl stop firewalld || true
#sudo /bin/systemctl disable firewalld || true

sudo sed -i 's/SELINUX=enforcing/SELINUX=disabled/' /etc/selinux/config
sudo setenforce 0

# region headers
# * author:     nutanix
# * version:    v1.0/20210504 -  version
# task_name:    InitializeDataDisk
# description:  configured /dev/sdb1 as an lvm volume, formats it
#               as ext4 and mounts it in /data, also configuring
#               fstab.               
# output vars:  none
# dependencies: none
# endregion

sudo yum install -y lvm2
printf 'o\nn\np\n\n\n\nt\n8e\nw' | sudo fdisk /dev/sdb
sudo pvcreate /dev/sdb1
sudo vgcreate vg0 /dev/sdb1
sudo lvcreate -n data -l 100%FREE vg0
sudo mkfs.ext4 /dev/vg0/data
sudo mkdir /data
sudo mount /dev/vg0/data /data
echo '/dev/mapper/vg0-data /data ext4 defaults 0 0' | sudo tee -a /etc/fstab

# region headers
# * author:     nutanix
# * version:    v1.1/20241113
# task_name:    ApplyLinuxUpdates
# description:  fixes repo URLs for EOL CentOS 7 and applies updates.
# endregion

# Fix outdated CentOS repos (EOL)
sudo sed -i 's|mirror.centos.org/centos/\$releasever|vault.centos.org/7.9.2009|g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-Base.repo
sudo sed -i 's|mirrorlist=|#mirrorlist=|g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-Base.repo
sudo sed -i 's|#baseurl=|baseurl=|g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-Base.repo

sudo yum clean all
sudo yum makecache

# Apply updates
sudo yum update -y
sudo yum upgrade -y

# region headers
# * author:     nutanix
# * version:    v1.1/20251113
# * task_name:  InstallNgt
# * description: Installs Nutanix Guest Tools on CentOS 7 VMs
# output vars:  none
# dependencies: none
# endregion

# Wait for the NGT ISO to attach
sleep 15

# Ensure yum is available and install tar if not present
sudo yum -y install tar

# Create mount point if it doesn’t exist
sudo mkdir -p /media

# Mount the NGT ISO (read-only mount is expected)
sudo mount /dev/sr0 /media

# Run the NGT installer script
sudo /media/installer/linux/install_ngt.py

# Optional: print recent NGT installation logs for debugging
sudo journalctl -u ngt_guest_agent_upgrade -n 50 --no-pager || true

#!/bin/bash
# Install Nginx on CentOS
set -e

echo "Starting Nginx installation on CentOS..."

# Enable EPEL repository (required for Nginx)
sudo yum install -y epel-release

# Install Nginx
sudo yum install -y nginx

# Enable and start Nginx service
sudo systemctl enable nginx
sudo systemctl start nginx

echo "Nginx installation completed successfully."
echo "Access your server via http://<VM-IP>"

echo "@@{dynamic_cred.username}@@"
echo "@@{dynamicvar_5}@@"
echo "@@{dynamicvar_4}@@"
echo "@@{dynamicvar_3}@@"
echo "@@{dynamicvar_2}@@"
echo "@@{dynamicvar_1}@@"

variable_list.name: vm_name
variable_list.description: Enter the hostname to give to this Linux virtual machine (max 15 characters). This will also be the VM name in the hypervisor.
variable_list.label: Hostname

variable_list.name: vm_requester_name
variable_list.description: This is used in the email notification and will also be part of the application instance definition for future references.
variable_list.label: Your full name

variable_list.name: requester_email
variable_list.description: This will be used to send you a notification with the provisioned VM details, including credentials.
variable_list.label: Your email address

variable_list.name: public_key
variable_list.description: Paste here your SSL public key
